---
title: "Capstone"
format: html
---

```{r echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='hide'}

library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(janitor)
library(here)
library(data.table)
library(dplyr)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)
library(slider)
library(fixest)
library(broom)
library(gt)
library(slider)
library(fixest)
```

```{r echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='hide'}
library(data.table)
# install.packages('R.utils')
data <- fread(here("data", "raw", "cps_00001.csv.gz"))

```

```{r echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='hide'}
analysis <- data[
  PAIDHOUR == 2 &              # Paid hourly
  UHRSWORKORG < 999
]
```

```{r echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='hide'}
state_raw <- read_excel(
  here("data", "raw", "mw_state_monthly.xlsx")
)

```

```{r echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='hide'}
state_mw <- state_raw %>%
  mutate(
    year  = as.integer(str_extract(`Monthly Date`, "^\\d{4}")),
    month = as.integer(str_extract(`Monthly Date`, "(?<=m)\\d+"))
  )

state_mw <- state_mw %>%
  filter(year >= 2010, year <= 2019)

state_mw <- state_mw %>%
  mutate(
    # Use the higher of the federal and state minimum wage as the effective minimum wage
    mw_effective = pmax(`Monthly Federal Minimum`, `Monthly State Minimum`, na.rm = TRUE),
    mw_effective = ifelse(is.infinite(mw_effective), NA_real_, mw_effective)
  )

state_mw_clean <- state_mw %>%
  select(
    STATEFIP = `State FIPS Code`,
    year,
    month,
    mw_effective
  )

analysis <- analysis %>%
  left_join(
    state_mw_clean,
    by = c("STATEFIP" = "STATEFIP",
           "YEAR"     = "year",
           "MONTH"    = "month")
  )

# Quick data-quality checks for the minimum-wage panel
state_mw %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    n_all_na = sum(is.na(`Monthly Federal Minimum`) & is.na(`Monthly State Minimum`)),
    n_mw_neg_inf = sum(is.infinite(mw_effective) & mw_effective < 0)
  )

# (a) Check whether the state-month key is unique in the minimum-wage table
state_mw_clean %>%
  count(STATEFIP, year, month) %>%
  filter(n > 1) %>%
  arrange(desc(n)) %>%
  head(20)

# (b) Identify state-months in CPS that are missing from the minimum-wage table
analysis %>%
  distinct(STATEFIP, YEAR, MONTH) %>%
  anti_join(state_mw_clean, by = c("STATEFIP"="STATEFIP","YEAR"="year","MONTH"="month")) %>%
  head(30)

# (c) Inspect the share of missing effective minimum wage after the join, by state
analysis %>%
  group_by(STATEFIP) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    share_mw_na = mean(is.na(mw_effective))
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(share_mw_na)) %>%
  head(20)
```

```{r echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='hide'}
analysis %>%
  summarise(
    share_missing_mw = mean(is.na(mw_effective))
  )
analysis %>%
  group_by(STATEFIP, YEAR) %>%
  summarise(n_mw = n_distinct(mw_effective)) %>%
  summarise(max(n_mw))
```

```{r echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='hide'}
analysis <- analysis %>%
  mutate(
    wage_gap = HOURWAGE - mw_effective
  )

analysis <- analysis %>%
  mutate(
    wage_ratio = HOURWAGE / mw_effective
  )

analysis <- analysis %>%
  mutate(
    wage_bin = case_when(
      wage_ratio < 1.00                         ~ "Below MW",
      wage_ratio >= 1.00 & wage_ratio < 1.10   ~ "MW to 10% above",
      wage_ratio >= 1.10 & wage_ratio < 1.25   ~ "10% to 25% above",
      wage_ratio >= 1.25 & wage_ratio < 1.50   ~ "25% to 50% above",
      TRUE                                      ~ "50%+ above"
    )
  )

# Check missingness and infinite values in wage measures
analysis %>%
  summarise(
    share_hourwage_na = mean(is.na(HOURWAGE)),
    share_mw_na       = mean(is.na(mw_effective)),
    share_ratio_na    = mean(is.na(wage_ratio)),
    share_ratio_inf   = mean(is.infinite(wage_ratio))
  )

```

```{r echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='hide'}


# ===== 0) Restrict to 2010-2019 and sort the minimum-wage panel by state-month =====
mw_panel <- state_mw_clean %>%
  filter(year >= 2010, year <= 2019) %>%
  arrange(STATEFIP, year, month) %>%
  group_by(STATEFIP) %>%
  mutate(
    mw_lag  = lag(mw_effective),
    d_mw    = mw_effective - mw_lag,
    pct_chg = (mw_effective / mw_lag) - 1
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# Summary counts of state-months and minimum-wage increase events
mw_panel %>%
  summarise(
    n_state_month = n(),
    n_raise_month = sum(d_mw > 0, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_states_with_raise = n_distinct(STATEFIP[d_mw > 0])
  )

mw_panel %>%
  filter(d_mw > 0) %>%
  count(STATEFIP, sort = TRUE) %>%
  head(20)

# Optional: inspect pct_chg for extreme values or missingness
summary(mw_panel$pct_chg)

# ===== 1) Count ΔMW > 0 events and >5% increases by state =====
mw_counts_by_state <- mw_panel %>%
  filter(!is.na(mw_lag)) %>%  # The first observation within each state has no lag
  group_by(STATEFIP) %>%
  summarise(
    n_pos_change = sum(d_mw > 0, na.rm = TRUE),        # Number of increases of any size
    n_gt5_change = sum(pct_chg > 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),  # Number of increases above 5%
    n_any_change = sum(d_mw != 0, na.rm = TRUE),       # Number of nonzero changes, including decreases
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# ===== 2) Distribution of increase counts: any increase and >5% increases =====
dist_pos <- mw_counts_by_state %>%
  count(n_pos_change, name = "num_states") %>%
  arrange(n_pos_change)

dist_gt5 <- mw_counts_by_state %>%
  count(n_gt5_change, name = "num_states") %>%
  arrange(n_gt5_change)

cat("\n=== Distribution: number of months with ΔMW > 0 (2010-2019) ===\n")
print(dist_pos, n = Inf)

cat("\n=== Distribution: number of months with percentage change > 5% (2010-2019) ===\n")
print(dist_gt5, n = Inf)

# ===== 3) States with only small increases and no >5% events =====
states_only_small <- mw_counts_by_state %>%
  filter(n_pos_change > 0, n_gt5_change == 0) %>%
  arrange(desc(n_pos_change))

cat("\n=== States with at least one increase but NO increase above 5% ===\n")
cat("Count of such states:", nrow(states_only_small), "\n")
print(states_only_small, n = Inf)

# Optional: states with no increases at all, as a sanity check
states_never_increase <- mw_counts_by_state %>%
  filter(n_pos_change == 0)

cat("\n=== States with ZERO increases (2010-2019) ===\n")
cat("Count of such states:", nrow(states_never_increase), "\n")
print(states_never_increase, n = Inf)

```
```{r echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='hide'}
write_rds(mw_panel, here("data", "processed", "mw_panel.rds"))

# mw_panel already contains mw_effective, mw_lag, d_mw, and pct_chg
events <- mw_panel %>%
  filter(!is.na(mw_lag), d_mw > 0) %>%
  transmute(
    STATEFIP,
    year,
    month,
    Te = sprintf("%04d-%02d", year, month),
    event_id = paste0(sprintf("%02d", STATEFIP), "_", Te),  # Stable event identifier
    delta_pct = pct_chg,
    delta_log = log(mw_effective) - log(mw_lag)
  )

# Check how many events each state experiences
events_by_state <- events %>%
  count(STATEFIP, name = "n_events") %>%
  arrange(desc(n_events))

summary(events_by_state$n_events)
head(events_by_state, 10)


```

```{r echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='hide'}
mw_changes <- mw_panel %>%
  filter(!is.na(mw_lag)) %>%
  transmute(
    STATEFIP,
    year,
    month,
    change_flag = as.integer(d_mw > 0)
  )

events2 <- events %>%
  mutate(Te_date = as.Date(sprintf("%04d-%02d-01", year, month))) %>%
  group_by(STATEFIP) %>%
  arrange(Te_date) %>%
  mutate(
    prev_Te_date = lag(Te_date),
    next_Te_date = lead(Te_date)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

event_window <- events2 %>%
  select(
    event_id,
    treated_state = STATEFIP,
    Te_date,
    prev_Te_date,
    next_Te_date,
    delta_pct,
    delta_log
  ) %>%
  tidyr::expand_grid(tau = -12:12) %>%
  mutate(
    cal_date = Te_date %m+% months(tau),
    year = year(cal_date),
    month = month(cal_date)
  ) %>%
  # Truncate each event window at the previous and next minimum-wage increases
  filter(
    (is.na(prev_Te_date) | cal_date > prev_Te_date) &
    (is.na(next_Te_date) | cal_date < next_Te_date)
  ) %>%
  select(event_id, treated_state, Te_date, tau, year, month, delta_pct, delta_log)

states_all <- mw_panel %>%
  distinct(STATEFIP)

event_state_month <- event_window %>%
  crossing(states_all) %>%
  left_join(mw_changes, by = c("STATEFIP", "year", "month")) %>%
  mutate(change_flag = ifelse(is.na(change_flag), 0L, change_flag))

elig_states <- event_state_month %>%
  group_by(event_id, STATEFIP, treated_state) %>%
  summarise(
    any_increase_in_window = any(change_flag == 1),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    is_treated_state = (STATEFIP == treated_state),
    # Always keep the treated state because the event window has already been trimmed
    # Control states must have no minimum-wage increases anywhere within the same window
    eligible = is_treated_state | (!is_treated_state & !any_increase_in_window)
  ) %>%
  filter(eligible) %>%
  select(event_id, STATEFIP)

stacked_state_month_clean <- event_state_month %>%
  inner_join(elig_states, by = c("event_id", "STATEFIP")) %>%
  mutate(treated = as.integer(STATEFIP == treated_state)) %>%
  select(
    event_id, STATEFIP, year, month, tau, treated_state, treated,
    delta_pct, delta_log
  )

# Basic checks on the stacked state-month panel
stacked_state_month_clean %>%
  summarise(
    n_rows = n(),
    n_events = n_distinct(event_id),
    n_events_with_treated = n_distinct(event_id[treated == 1])
  )

write_rds(
  stacked_state_month_clean,
  here("data", "processed", "stacked_state_month_clean.rds")
)
```

```{r echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='hide'}

mw_base <- stacked_state_month_clean %>%
  filter(tau >= -3, tau <= -1) %>%
  left_join(
    mw_panel %>% select(STATEFIP, year, month, mw_effective),
    by = c("STATEFIP", "year", "month")
  ) %>%
  group_by(event_id, STATEFIP) %>%
  summarise(
    mw_base = mean(mw_effective, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

stacked_micro0 <- analysis %>%
  inner_join(
    stacked_state_month_clean,
    by = c("STATEFIP", "YEAR" = "year", "MONTH" = "month")
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    ym = YEAR * 100 + MONTH,
    tau = as.integer(tau),
    treated = as.integer(treated)
  ) %>%
  left_join(mw_base, by = c("event_id", "STATEFIP")) %>%
  filter(!is.na(mw_base), !is.na(HOURWAGE), HOURWAGE > 0)

# Trim the sample using the pre-period minimum wage so the threshold does not shift at tau = 0
stacked_micro_clean <- stacked_micro0 %>%
  mutate(ratio_base = HOURWAGE / mw_base) %>%
  filter(ratio_base >= 0.75, ratio_base <= 1.50)

write_rds(stacked_micro_clean, here("data", "processed", "stacked_micro_clean.rds"))
```

```{r echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='hide'}

# 0) Start from the stacked micro data before applying the near-worker restriction
rep_ct <- stacked_state_month_clean %>%
  filter(tau >= -12, tau <= 12) %>%
  distinct(event_id, STATEFIP, year, month) %>%
  count(STATEFIP, year, month, name = "rep")

dat_main0 <- stacked_micro_clean %>%
  left_join(rep_ct, by = c("STATEFIP", "YEAR" = "year", "MONTH" = "month")) %>%
  mutate(
    rep = ifelse(is.na(rep), 1L, rep),
    EARNWT_adj = EARNWT / rep
  ) %>%
  filter(tau >= -12, tau <= 12)

write_rds(dat_main0, here("data", "processed", "dat_main0.rds"))

# 1) Compute pre-policy cell-level wages using observations with tau <= -1
cell_vars <- c("event_id", "STATEFIP", "OCC2010", "IND", "EDUC", "SEX")  # Keep the cell definition relatively coarse

cell_pre <- dat_main0 %>%
  filter(tau >= -12, tau <= -1) %>%
  group_by(across(all_of(cell_vars))) %>%
  summarise(
    w_pre = weighted.mean(HOURWAGE, EARNWT_adj, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# 2) Define the predetermined near-worker sample based on pre-policy cell wages
dat_cell <- dat_main0 %>%
  left_join(cell_pre, by = cell_vars) %>%
  mutate(
    ratio_pre = w_pre / mw_base,
    near_cell = !is.na(ratio_pre) & ratio_pre >= 0.8 & ratio_pre <= 1.2
  )

dat_cell_near <- dat_cell %>%
  filter(near_cell) %>%
  group_by(event_id) %>%
  filter(
    any(treated == 1) &
    any(treated == 1 & tau <= -1) &
    any(treated == 1 & tau >= 0)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

write_rds(dat_cell_near, here("data", "processed", "dat_cell_near.rds"))

```
